- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.

- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.

retrieved_doc_snippets: The Good Fight | The Good Fight is an American legal and political drama television series produced for CBS's streaming service CBS All Access. It is CBS All Access' first original scripted series. The series is the sequel to "The Good Wife" which was created by Robert King and Michelle King. The first season contains 10 episodes, and it premiered on February 19, 2017, with the first episode airing on CBS and the latter nine episodes airing on CBS All Access. The series was initially planned to  ...[truncated]

Cush Jumbo | Cush Jumbo (born 23 September 1985) is an English actress and writer. She wrote and starred in a one-woman show, "Josephine and I". In 2011, she won the Ian Charleson Award for young actors as Rosalind in "As You Like It" and received an Olivier nomination in 2012 as Mark Antony in an all-female cast of "Julius Caesar". She played attorney Lucca Quinn in the seventh season of the U.S. drama series "The Good Wife" on CBS and its spinoff "The Good Fight" on CBS All Access.

Rose Leslie | Rose Eleanor Arbuthnot-Leslie (born 9 February 1987), known professionally as Rose Leslie, is a Scottish actress. After winning a Scottish BAFTA for Best Acting Performance for her role in "New Town", she rose to fame as Gwen Dawson in the ITV drama series "Downton Abbey" and as Ygritte in the HBO fantasy series "Game of Thrones". She currently stars as Maia Rindell in the CBS All Access legal drama "The Good Fight".

CBS All Access | CBS All Access is an over-the-top subscription streaming video on demand service owned and operated by CBS. It offers original content, content newly aired on CBS's broadcast properties, and content from CBS's library, along with live streams of the local CBS affiliate's main channel, where available. s of February 2017 , the service has nearly 1.5 million subscribers.

Diane Lockhart | Diane Lockhart is a character of CBS television series "The Good Wife" and is portrayed by Christine Baranski, who has received positive reviews for her performance, such as being nominated for six consecutive Primetime Emmy Awards for Outstanding Supporting Actress in a Drama Series. The character returned as the main protagonist on the sequel series "The Good Fight".

UFC All Access | UFC All Access was a reality television show which aired on Spike TV. Hosted by Rachelle Leah, "UFC All Access" went behind the scenes into the lives of Mixed Martial Arts fighters in the Ultimate Fighting Championship as they trained for their upcoming bouts. It usually aired during the week of a major UFC pay-per-view event. Spike TV stopped production on the series.
